summary: Gruber dismantles a Bloomberg report claiming Apple reduced Face ID accuracy to speed up iPhone X production, noting that Apple issued an unusually strong on-the-record denial. He then builds a broader case that the entire narrative around Face ID being Apple's 'Plan B' — pushed by analysts and reporters throughout 2017 — was wrong, citing his own sources who say Apple committed to Face ID over a year before the iPhone X announcement and locked down the hardware design before January 2017. He argues these reports either reflect deliberate FUD from supply chain competitors or simply the months-long lag between Apple's internal decisions and when information reaches outside analysts.
key_points: Apple issued an extraordinary on-the-record denial calling Bloomberg's Face ID accuracy claim 'completely false,' breaking their usual no-comment policy
Bloomberg's own story undermines itself by admitting 'it's not clear how much the new specs will reduce the technology's efficacy' while running a headline suggesting Face ID was compromised
Multiple sources within Apple told Gruber that Face ID was chosen over Touch ID more than a year before the iPhone X announcement, and hardware was locked before January 2017
A parade of analyst reports and news stories throughout 2017 wrongly claimed Apple was still deciding between Touch ID and Face ID as late as summer, when the decision was nearly a year old
The rumor pipeline likely reflects either deliberate supply-chain FUD from competitors or a 9+ month lag between Apple's internal decisions and when they reach analysts
money_quotes: It is extraordinary for Apple to issue a blanket 'this is completely false' statement on any news story.
If this weren't merely clickbait, they'd be able to say how well it actually works.
This was a nine-month-old decision that Bloomberg reported in the present tense.
The idea that they were still 'working this out' in June is a joke.
That's a lot of 'well-placed sources' for a bullshit story.
If Apple had really been dithering over Touch ID-embedded-in-the-display vs. Face ID in June of this year, iPhone X wouldn't be hitting the market until 2018.
FUD is one of the oldest tricks in the book.
Their sources are so far removed from the halls of Cupertino that they mistake old news for new news.
key_insight: The iPhone rumor ecosystem consistently misreported Apple's Face ID timeline by nearly a year, revealing how far removed analysts and reporters are from Apple's actual engineering decisions — and how easily supply-chain noise gets packaged as authoritative reporting.
